name: Buttermilk Pound Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Have all ingredients at room temperature (70°F).
Adjust oven racks to divide the oven into thirds; preheat
the oven to 325°F. Spray a 5-cup loaf pan, bundt pan,
or tube pan with nonstick cooking spray.
2. In a small bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder,
baking soda, and salt; sift together and set aside.
3. In another small bowl, whisk together egg and egg
whites; set aside.
4. In a small bowl, combine buttermilk and vanilla;
set aside.
5. Cut margarine and butter into 1/4-inch chunks and
place in a large bowl of an electric mixer. Beat at
medium speed until softened, about 1 minute. Gradually
add sugar, beating constantly, until smooth, about 3
minutes. Gradually dribble in beaten eggs, beating at
medium-high speed for 2-3 minutes.
6. On low speed, beat in one-third of the reserved flour
mixture, scraping sides of the bowl. Beat on medium-
high speed, gradually dribbling in half of the buttermilk
mixture, scraping sides of the bowl. Beat on low speed,
involving half of the remaining flour mixture. Beat on
medium-high speed, incorporating the rest of the buttermilk
mixture, scraping sides of the bowl. Beat on low speed,
incorporating the remaining flour mixture until well
combined (the batter may look curdled).
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake until
the cake starts to pull away from the sides of the pan,
the top is golden brown, and a toothpick inserted in the
center comes out clean, 35-40 minutes for bundt or tube
pan, or 65-70 minutes for loaf pan.
8. Cool on a rack 10-15 minutes; invert the pan and unmold
onto the rack to cool completely. Cut into 12 slices.
